fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Washington Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Washington - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity washington, how to transfer physical therapist license to washington, physical therapist compact washington, washington physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Washington DOH — Physical Therapy
board.mailing_address:
Department of Health
Physical Therapy Board
PO Box 47852
Olympia, WA 98504-7852

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Washington.

steps.title: Create SecureAccess Washington (SAW) Account
steps.description: Access the Washington Department of Health website for Physical Therapists and click 'Apply for a License'. You will be prompted to create a SecureAccess Washington (SAW) account if you do not have one.

steps.title: Add HPQA Online Services to SAW
steps.description: Log into your SAW account and add the Health Professions Quality Assurance (HPQA) Online Services to your dashboard. This is where you will manage and submit your application.

steps.title: Submit Online Application and Fee
steps.description: Complete the detailed online application form within HPQA Online Services, providing personal, educational, and examination history. Pay the initial application fee of $225.

steps.title: Send Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your accredited physical therapy program to be sent directly to the Washington State Board of Physical Therapy. Also, ensure your NPTE scores are sent (if not already on file).

steps.title: Complete Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Take and pass the Washington State Physical Therapy Jurisprudence Examination, which is typically an online, open-book test covering state laws and rules related to physical therapy practice.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprint and Background Check
steps.description: Follow instructions provided during the application process to complete the required fingerprint-based background check through a state-approved vendor.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Washington physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $225. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Washington have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Washington participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Washington without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Washington?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Washington while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Washington
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Washington, you will use the Health Professions Quality Assurance (HPQA) Online Services, accessed through SecureAccess Washington (SAW), starting from the Department of Health website.

official_links.portal_name: HPQA Online Services via SecureAccess Washington (SAW)
official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Washington Department of Health Physical Therapist page and click 'Apply for a License'. You will be directed to create a SecureAccess Washington (SAW) account if you don't have one, and then add the Health Professions Quality Assurance (HPQA) Online Services to your SAW account to begin your application.
